V-M-072.scope: Phase-55 premium Matrix art-direction verification for shared user/admin visual tokens, typography, density, logo rules, reduced-motion readability, and protected runtime boundaries.

module-checks.check-7: git diff --name-only HEAD -- install.sh docker-compose.yml nginx deploy deploy/mtproto-de-compose.yml .env.example must be empty

verification-matrix.target-1: CSS exposes premium tokens for Matrix green, cyan, magenta, amber, red, neutral, deep-black surfaces, focus rings, data surfaces, chart accents, and status colors without becoming a one-note hue theme.
verification-matrix.target-2: Typography rules keep display, UI, KPI, terminal/data, and email-adjacent scales compact; letter-spacing remains 0 for operational text and no viewport-width font scaling is introduced.
verification-matrix.target-3: Density rules reject nested cards, oversized radius classes, large decorative hero/card grids, decorative orb/radial backgrounds, and excessive glass padding on operational surfaces.
verification-matrix.target-4: Logo rules document approved source assets from /home/truffle/Загрузки/ДЛЯ КРОТА/logos, minimum readable sizes, clear-space, contrast, favicon/email-logo boundaries, and no stretched bitmap usage.
verification-matrix.target-5: Chrome screenshot audit covers 360x740, 390x844, 430x932, 768x1024, 1024x768, and 1440x900 for user login/admin login or equivalent shared shell routes.
verification-matrix.target-6: Reduced-motion screenshots prove Matrix identity remains visible while flicker, glitch, and transform-heavy effects are disabled or simplified.
verification-matrix.target-7: Matrix canvas and overlay layers remain pointer-events none, never intercept scrolling/focus/clicks, and degrade to a readable static background if canvas context is unavailable.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: User and admin builds pass after premium token adoption.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: Phase-55 smoke proves balanced palette, compact density, focus-visible accessibility, reduced-motion policy, logo usage rules, and protected-surface isolation.
success-scenarios.scenario-3: Screenshot review confirms premium Matrix/cyberpunk style without hiding primary auth, VPN, MTProto, tariff, or admin workflows.
success-scenarios.scenario-4: No backend API, auth/session, billing, VPN, MTProto, deploy, install, nginx, docker-compose, or runtime topology changes appear in Phase-55 diff.

failure-scenarios.failure-1: Text overlaps icons, controls, status badges, chart labels, rows, or adjacent content on phone/tablet/desktop screenshots.
failure-scenarios.failure-2: Palette collapses into a single green, purple, blue/slate, beige, brown/orange, or heavy gradient theme.
failure-scenarios.failure-3: Large heroes, nested cards, decorative orbs, bokeh blobs, oversized rounded panels, or marketing-style sections enter operational route surfaces.
failure-scenarios.failure-4: Reduced-motion mode still runs flicker/glitch/transform-heavy effects or the fallback loses visual identity/readability.
failure-scenarios.failure-5: Phase-55 diff touches deploy/install/nginx/docker-compose/runtime surfaces or changes backend data/API behavior.

telemetry-requirements.requirement-1: Smoke script prints deterministic markers above without secrets, user emails, tokens, proxy secrets, payment IDs, or Resend keys.
telemetry-requirements.requirement-2: Visual screenshot artifacts may be local-only; if stored, paths must not include credentials or operator server access material.
telemetry-requirements.requirement-3: Console markers from MatrixBackground remain non-sensitive and limited to canvas lifecycle/reduced-motion fallback visibility.

phase-gates.gate-2: Existing M-038/M-069/M-070/M-071 contracts remain satisfied after token adoption.
phase-gates.gate-3: Frontend/admin builds, screenshot matrix, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff check, and protected deploy/install guard pass before Phase-55 can be marked done-local.

V-M-072.autonomous-execution-safety: Safe for local autonomous execution because Phase-55 is restricted to frontend visual tokens, art-direction documentation, and smoke verification; any deploy/install/runtime/backend change is a hard verification failure and must stop execution.
